danger_signals.visual_form: Screen zoom-in as opposing ships converge on single-screen battlefield
Crew count reduction in cockpit display; visual degradation of ship status
danger_signals.meaning: Player vessel sustaining losses; approaching destruction threshold

danger_signals.visual_form: Battery meter drain during weapon/ability usage
danger_signals.meaning: Tactical vulnerability; limited ability to execute attacks or maneuvers

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy faction ship icons positioned on rotating galactic map during turn-based strategy
danger_signals.meaning: Territorial threat; enemy expansion and territorial control

danger_signals.visual_form: Planet gravity well positioned on combat arena; visual trajectory warnings
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; potential involuntary ship destruction

safety_signals.visual_form: Colonized planet markers on star map; faction-colored world indicators
safety_signals.meaning: Resource generation point; crew replenishment source; territorial security

safety_signals.visual_form: Faction homeworld/base marker on rotating star map (primary strategic objective)
safety_signals.meaning: Command center; faction power center; defensive stronghold

safety_signals.visual_form: Battery meter refilling during non-combat periods; automatic regeneration indicator
safety_signals.meaning: Weapon systems recovering; tactical capability restoration

safety_signals.visual_form: Crew counter increasing when docked at friendly colonized worlds
safety_signals.meaning: Health/damage recovery; force replenishment; combat readiness restoration

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ur-Quan Dreadnaught flagship as faction flagship; Ur-Quan as faction namesake
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ships within hierarchy display faction-specific design language and cockpit animations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occupied/controlled planets on star map display Ur-Quan faction allegiance coloring

faction_indicators.color_identity: Visually distinct from Ur-Quan Hierarchy (defensive/peaceful faction)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Multiple alien species represented; human contingent; diverse ship designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Alliance ships use different visual aesthetic than Hierarchy counterparts
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Allied planets display faction allegiance coloring; multiple species territory markers on map

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Planet color/allegiance indicator changes on star map from one faction to another
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Resource flow redirects to controlling faction; strategic advantage manifests in subsequent turns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ship icons on star map representing player's operational vessels; faction-specific ship silhouettes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Available ship options for combat reflect current fleet roster; destroyed ships removed from tactical pool

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increasing frequency of enemy ship encounters on star map; denser opposing fleet positioning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiple consecutive combat engagements; accelerated territorial contestation; reduced safe zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Colony quantity and distribution on star map indicates faction economic capacity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ship purchase options and crew replenishment capacity reflects resource accumulation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Star base surrounded by enemy-controlled territories; minimal friendly colony presence on map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited strategic options; forced defensive posture; high-risk combat scenarios

environmental_storytelling.example: Yehat Terminator's forcefield ability and visual design communicates defensive doctrine; VUX Intruder's limpet launcher reflects encumbrance/slowing tactics. Distinct cockpit animations (barking Chenjesu drones vs bellowing Ur-Quan Dreadnaught) establish alien species personality and combat philosophy
Visual distribution of faction-colored planets on rotating galactic map communicates strategic positions, contested regions, and balance of power without explicit UI text
Planets positioned as environmental hazards in combat arenas reinforce sci-fi naval combat setting; gravity well mechanics communicate galactic-scale physics constraints
Unique character design and animation for each alien species/ship in cockpit display reinforces distinct faction identity and species cultural differentiation
Expensive ships visually appear more formidable and possess advanced abilities, communicating economic investment and military hierarchy without explicit stat display

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Ship responds at full tactical capability; all weapons and abilities available; increased durability in combat
Visual ship degradation; reduced combat effectiveness; vulnerability to single hit destruction
Aggressive colonization attempt; territorial expansion maneuver; collision likely with player expansion
Defensive evasion; reduced weapon output; momentum-based movement reliance
Strategic nexus; primary objective marker; defensive anchor point; controls resource allocation

notes: Star Control communicates world-state primarily through visual representation on the rotating star map (turn-based strategy layer) and cockpit animation/ship aesthetics during real-time combat. Faction identity relies on color-coding, ship design language, and unique audio signatures rather than explicit text labels. Danger signals emerge from spatial proximity mechanics (enemy approach), resource depletion (battery/crew), and territorial encirclement. The game uses minimal UI text, relying instead on visual affordances and design language to communicate strategic and tactical information. Sound design (barking drones, bellowing Dreadnaught) reinforces faction personality.
